items.title: DeFi protocol Summer.fi halts Lazy Summer vaults after $6 million exploit
items.description: The protocol’s SUMR token fell by over 18% after the incident.

items.title: Cantor says Strategy's recovery hinges on restoring STRC to par
items.description: Bringing Strategy's preferred shares back to $100 is key to restarting Strategy's capital engine, the bank said, with repeated management actions expected to support both preferred and common shareholders.

items.title: Bitmine added another $74 million in ether as Tom Lee bets on Clarity Act boost
items.description: The second-largest digital asset treasury increased its buying pace, while Strategy sold a chunk of its bitcoin holdings.

items.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy dramatically ups pace of bitcoin sales, raising $216 million
items.description: The company sold 3,588 bitcoin last week to replenish its dollar reserves for dividends on its preferred stock.

items.title: Russia's largest bank plans crypto wallet launch as Moscow clears market path
items.description: Services will integrate into "Sberbank Online" and "SberInvestments" once the "On Digital Currency and Digital Rights" bill takes effect in September.

items.title: SpaceX's Nasdaq 100 inclusion comes with a historical warning
items.description: The largest IPO ever enters the Nasdaq 100. Previous additions like Palantir and Strategy saw volatility after the index inclusion rather than the start of new rallies.

items.title: U.S. inflation outlook underpins bitcoin bulls after best week since March
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 6, 2026

items.title: Crypto bounces back from the brink as altcoin optimism returns despite pockets of weakness
items.description: BTC holds at $62,800 after last week's brush with $58,000, while LIT surges 50% and the Altcoin Season indicator hits its highest reading in three months.

items.title: FCA warns of major shakeup as AI agents meet tokenized money
items.description: The FCA’s vision for agentic AI points toward a financial system where programmable money and tokenized assets could play a much larger role.

items.title: Crypto hacks fell 47% in H1 but ecosystem is no safer: CertiK
items.description: Crypto exploits rose 59% quarter-on-quarter to $807.5 million in Q2, due partly to the KelpDAO and Drift Protocol exploits, which came at the hands of North Korean hackers.

items.title: Strategy sells 3,588 Bitcoin for $216M to fund dividends, keeps $2.55B reserve intact
items.description: Strategy sold $216 million in Bitcoin to fund its dividend payments, as wealth manager Bernstein maintained its year-end Bitcoin price target of $150,000.

items.title: Belgian regulator flags 6 unauthorized crypto providers after MiCA deadline
items.description: Belgium’s FSMA warned consumers about six crypto-asset service providers it added to a fraudulent CASP list days after the EU’s MiCA transitional period expired.

items.title: $60.4K Becomes 'most important area': Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
items.description: Bitcoin saw its highest levels in nearly two weeks at the weekly close, but bulls needed a push beyond $65,000 for real trend inflection, said the latest BTC price analysis.

items.title: South Korea weighs action against Polymarket over gambling concerns
items.description: South Korea’s media and communications review body will hear from Polymarket before deciding whether to issue a corrective request against the prediction market platform.

items.title: Dormant $1.9M Bitcoin tied to New York lawsuit moves after nearly 15 years
items.description: A Bitcoin address dormant for nearly 15 years moved $1.9 million in BTC as a New York lawsuit seeks ownership of thousands of inactive holdings.

items.title: Nigel Farage accepted gifts from crypto-linked fraudster: Report
items.description: Before he was an MP, Nigel Farage was reportedly gifted staff, security and other benefits from George Cottrell, a convicted fraudster involved in a crypto casino.

items.title: Trump rings opening bell at White House with raft of CEOs in first-of-its-kind market open
items.description: The Trump Accounts include a one-time $1,000 pilot program contribution from the U.S. Treasury Department for babies born from 2025 through 2028.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, as Xbox unit downsizes and plans to spin off four gaming studios
items.description: Microsoft is cutting jobs in its commercial business and its Xbox gaming group, where revenue has been shrinking.

items.title: Belgium set to appeal as FIFA reverses Balogun's World Cup suspension after Trump reportedly intervenes
items.description: Folarin Balogun was surprisingly cleared by FIFA to play for the United States Men's National Team in the World Cup against Belgium on Monday.

items.title: Lockheed Martin to buy naval defense firm Ultra Maritime for $3.45 billion
items.description: Ultra is owned by private equity firm Advent International, and specialises in anti-submarine technology.

items.title: Versant agrees to buy golf simulator company Full Swing for $530 million
items.description: The deal for Full Swing will expand Versant's nontraditional media assets in an effort to diversify revenue from cable television.

items.title: NATO tensions are ‘growing pains,’ U.S. ambassador says as Trump presses allies
items.description: U.S. Ambassador to NATO Matthew Whitaker said NATO's allies boosting defense spending under pressure from Trump reflects “growing pains,” not a crisis.

items.title: TeraWulf shares soar after Anthropic leases data center in Kentucky
items.description: TeraWulf is a crytpo mining company that has pivoted to AI data center infrastructure. Its stock is up more than 80% this year.

items.title: Record heat, crowds drive offseason boom in international travel
items.description: Airlines are extending flight schedules to maximize on lucrative international travel.

items.title: Bitcoin retreats from two-week high, slipping below $62,000
Bitcoin’s ongoing bear market has been ’rather comforting,’ analyst says

items.description: Alexander Stubb tells FT that long-range drone strikes have changed US views on the war and put Kyiv in ‘best’ position since 2022

items.title: Russia hammers Kyiv with missiles and drones on eve of Nato summit
items.description: Zelenskyy pleads with allies to provide Patriot interceptors to defend Ukraine

items.title: Lukashenko says Belarus will not join Putin’s war in Ukraine
items.description: Kyiv feared that Moscow was pressuring its closest ally to support war effort

items.description: The veteran investor has put himself at the centre of the global AI boom. Some think he now has too much control

items.title: Putin is running out of options
items.description: The Russian leader may want to escalate his war — but that is harder than it sounds

items.title: Why OpenAI and Anthropic may struggle to float
items.description: The costs of remaining at the frontier of AI are punishing, but the penalties for falling behind may be even worse

items.title: Fifa ‘crossed a red line’ by reversing US star striker’s suspension, Uefa says
items.description: Governing body lifted a one-match ban given to US striker Folarin Balogun after a phone call from Donald Trump

items.title: Bellingham and Kane see England through after high drama at Azteca

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=So-8rVI3ZKk! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: How parents can take advantage of the ‘best summer-camp tax break in years’
items.description: For many kids, summer means camp. For many of their parents, that means financial juggling to pay for those camp experiences.

items.title: Trump rings Wall Street opening bells and says the stock market ‘is going to go through the roof’
items.description: Trump used the occasion to promote the launch of “Trump accounts,” which are special investment accounts for children under age 18.

items.title: As oil exits the ‘danger zone,’ here’s what history suggests happens next for stocks
items.description: The swift pullback in oil prices is a tailwind for the economy, says Evercore ISI

items.title: Global stocks may be headed for a summer swoon. Why AI won’t be the only story that matters, JPMorgan says
items.description: Strategists at the investment bank outlined five key drivers for global equities in the months ahead

items.title: Your investing expectations are more than double the reality. Here is the tough truth.
items.description: Long-term real returns above 10% annualized are exceedingly rare.

items.title: AI hyperscalers are poised for a big comeback rally as chip gains cool, says Morgan Stanley
items.description: The once-hot semiconductor trade has seen a cooling in the last couple of weeks, which is not only likely to continue, but usher in a bumpy ride for the overall stock market.

items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. But that could sink your retirement plans.
items.description: More investors want their money to reflect their values. It feels righteous — but the reality is a lot messier.

items.title: I have no kids. Will I cause family drama by leaving different amounts to my nieces and nephews?
items.description: “Part of me would prefer to leave more to those for whom the money could make a meaningful difference.”

items.title: Germany to borrow €800bn for rearmament in historic shift
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s debt-fuelled spending not seen since German reunification

items.title: Honeywell spin-off targets AI supply chain with $14.5bn deal for Element
items.description: Deal will create a leading advanced materials company with a combined enterprise value of roughly $29bn

items.title: Nato backs Ukraine’s push to hit Russia harder, says Finnish president
items.description: Alexander Stubb tells FT that long-range drone strikes have changed US views on the war and put Kyiv in ‘best’ position since 2022

items.title: Money in politics, part 94
items.description: The US Supreme Court handed down a potentially transformative judgment on campaign finance last week

items.title: Millions to attend funeral procession for Iran supreme leader
items.description: Event in Tehran marks one of the largest public gatherings in country’s recent history

items.title: Lukashenko says Belarus will not join Putin’s war in Ukraine
items.description: Kyiv feared that Moscow was pressuring its closest ally to support war effort

items.title: Erdoğan rival shuttled between trials as Nato meets in Turkey
items.description: Jailed Istanbul mayor İmamoğlu sent to three separate criminal hearings as president readies security summit

items.title: The best exhibitions to see in London this weekend
items.description: The FT’s critics recommend the most compelling 2026 shows, from Anish Kapoor and Barbara Hepworth to Frida Kahlo to Zurbarán

items.title: Trump’s World Cup Intervention Roils the Soccer World, and Beyond
items.description: The U.S.-Belgium soccer game is turning out to be a huge deal even before it kicks off on Monday, thrusting politics into the spotlight.

items.title: Ships Are Moving In and Out of the Persian Gulf, Easing Oil Prices
items.description: A recovery in oil flows from the Persian Gulf and a pledge by OPEC Plus to pump more crude have put downward pressure on energy prices.

items.title: Comcast Adds Heft With Sky’s Deal for British Broadcaster ITV
items.description: The $2.1 billion purchase would combine two major British media outlets and help the Comcast-owned Sky compete with streaming giants.

items.title: Zyn’s Popularity Has Tobacco Companies Racing to Cash In
items.description: The makers of nicotine pouches are building new plants and expanding to meet demand. While influencers claim health benefits, experts warn the products can be highly addictive.

items.title: The Chinese Company That Could Start a Trade War With Europe
items.description: Nuctech’s rise from a Chinese state-backed start-up to a global border-security supplier has made it a lightning rod in trade and subsidy disputes.

items.title: ‘Minions’ Opens at No. 1 but Falls Far Short of Earlier Films in the Franchise
items.description: The movie generated $36.4 million in ticket sales in North America for the holiday weekend, a 62 percent decline from 2024, the last time a “Minions” movie was in theaters.

items.title: OPEC Plus Pledges to Pump More Even as Oil Prices Fall
items.description: The cartel’s decision comes as U.S.-Iran talks to end the war have eased pressure on oil supplies, even as broader market and political volatility persists.

items.title: Taylor Swift’s Wedding Became a Marketing Moment for Brands Big and Small
items.description: Some businesses flocked to social media using A.I.-generated content, while others deployed timely digital ads designed to capitalize on the frenzy surrounding this major celebrity event.
